stories.title: Dua Lipa calls Albanian protests against Jared Kushner-backed resort ‘inspiring’
stories.summary: Pop star, whose parents are Kosovan Albanian, expresses admiration for demonstrations, which are entering their sixth week Albania’s “flamingo revolution” has won its most prominent supporter yet after the pop star Dua Lipa expressed admiration for the protest movement against a Trump family-backed resort in the Balkan state. stories.title: Trump’s Nato remarks rang hollow – but he recognizes something real | Christopher S Chivvis
stories.summary: US political leaders must be more clear-eyed about our global alliances, without embracing his scorched-earth approach Donald Trump memorably took out a full-page advertisement in multiple newspapers in 1987 charging that America was carrying too much weight for its allies. In his first term he repeated this charge, threatening to withdraw from Nato and berating US allies around the world in the process. stories.title: Mahmoud Khalil sues Trump officials and pro-Israel groups over ‘conspiracy’ to target him
stories.summary: Lawsuit alleges administration coordinated with online surveillance groups to ‘criminalize solidarity with Palestine’ Mahmoud Khalil filed a lawsuit on Tuesday against Trump administration officials and several pro-Israel groups, accusing them of conspiring to target him and others as punishment for their support of Palestinian rights. stories.title: EU relaxes rules for smart glasses after US pressure
stories.summary: The European Commission on Tuesday exempted wearable tech from rules requiring removable batteries, clearing a major hurdle for Meta’s smart glasses to take on the EU market following pressure from the United States. Smart glasses are in European regulators’ and lawmakers’ crosshairs over privacy and surveillance concerns. stories.title: EU rejects Trump administration claims that ICC threatens US sovereignty
stories.summary: US government says it wants to ‘systematically disable’ The Hague-based international criminal court A spokesperson for the EU has pushed back against the Trump administration’s assertion that the international criminal court poses a threat to US sovereignty, a day after the US government said it would work to “systematically disable” a global tribunal that seeks to prosecute the perpetrators of the world’s gravest crimes. stories.title: ICE pauses vehicle stops after deadly shootings in Texas and Maine
stories.summary: Deaths of Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero and Lorenzo Salgado Araujo prompt calls for independent investigations Federal immigration officials have been instructed to stop pulling over vehicles until further notice, according to a homeland security source, following two recent deadly shootings in Texas and Maine during which officials shot and killed immigrants in vehicles. stories.title: EU prosecutor asks for police probe into subsidies for Babiš-linked Agrofert
stories.summary: The European Public Prosecutor’s Office has tasked Czech police with investigating the payment of EU subsidies to companies within Agrofert, an agro-chemical giant tied to Czech Prime Minister Andrej Babiš. In a letter dated June 19, European Delegated Prosecutor Daniela Bártíková said she “decided to initiate criminal proceedings … concerning funds provided from the EU budgets.” The letter, seen by POLITICO, was first reported by Seznam Zprávy . stories.title: Keir Starmer becomes first UK PM to receive France’s Légion d’honneur
stories.summary: Exclusive: Emmanuel Macron honours outgoing prime minister for leadership role in supporting Ukraine Keir Starmer has become the first UK prime minister to be presented with the Légion d’honneur by a French president, in recognition of his work with France on the security of Europe. stories.summary: Firework displays cancelled as Paris military parade asserts ‘France’s rearmament … and Europe’s strategic awakening’ Emmanuel Macron has presided over his final Bastille Day parade in Paris amid a searing heatwave and wildfires that forced authorities around the country to cancel traditional firework displays and balls celebrating France’s national day. stories.title: Foreign Office appoints first special envoy for Britons detained overseas
stories.summary: Alistair Burt will bring diplomatic clout to complex cases involving human rights violations and arbitrary detention The Foreign Office has appointed a special envoy for British citizens detained overseas, a new role to deal with “complex consular cases” such as that of Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe, the British-Iranian dual national who was imprisoned in Tehran for six years. stories.title: Man killed by semi after fleeing from immigration officials in Florida

stories.summary: Conviction over hiring by socialist-led council is one of a series of corruption claims facing Pedro Sánchez’s family The brother of Spain’s prime minister has been banned from holding public office for nine years after being found guilty of administrative misconduct relating to his hiring by a socialist-led council in the south-western region of Extremadura nine years ago. stories.title: Trump backtracks on plan to charge 20 percent fee for ships traveling through Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Tuesday walked back threats to charge a 20 percent fee on all cargo ships that pass through the Strait of Hormuz, the critical waterway that has been central to the renewed U.S.-Iran conflict. In  a post to Truth Social , Trump said the strait will be open to “ALL Ship traffic except for Iran” and the U.S. will implement a “FULL Blockade, but only on Ships coming to and from Iranian ports, or carrying anything have to do with Iranian cargo.” The

stories.title: Macron hands Starmer Légion d’honneur as parting gift
stories.summary: LONDON — French President Emmanuel Macron presented U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer with one of France’s highest honors — the Légion d’honneur — in recognition of his contribution to Europe’s security. Macron bestowed the award on Tuesday as the two leaders met in Paris for the latest session of the Coalition of the Willing, a gathering of allies led by Britain and France that has pledged to help secure a ceasefire in Ukraine. stories.title: ‘Anthropic doesn’t care about Europe’ — EU officials peeved after AI giant sends junior staffer to testify about safety
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — European policymakers criticized AI company Anthropic on Tuesday for failing to send a senior company official to address Brussels’ concerns about risks posed by advanced AI capabilities. Anthropic appointed Donny Greenberg, a technical employee who joined the company in April, to address the European Parliament via video from New York, rather than its head of public policy, Sarah Heck, as lawmakers had requested. “I’m a technical person, not a policy person,”

stories.title: MPs say death threats a daily reality in ‘increasing climate of abuse’
stories.summary: As Ann Widdecombe’s death raises concerns over security, politicians reveal extent of problem Jess Phillips has received so many death threats she has to remind herself not to be blase. One night she received more than 600 rape threats. In 2019, a man forced his way into her office. stories.summary: One social media user described the opening of its first outlet in Monterrey as ‘like the dog teaching a duck to fly’ A US chain’s plan to sell its version of Mexican food to Mexicans with a first branch south of the border has prompted bemusement, skepticism and anger among local people. stories.summary: Andrew Bailey warns that US will not be able to achieve its ambitions alone The Bank of England governor has called for international cooperation to tackle growing AI threats, warning that the US and Trump administration would not be able to achieve their ambitions alone. Andrew Bailey’s comments come weeks after the US president, Donald Trump, temporarily banned foreigners from using Anthropic’s powerful Claude Mythos model. Continue reading...

stories.title: Darline Graham, sister of Lindsey Graham, sworn in to fulfill his Senate term
stories.summary: Lindsey Graham’s younger sister appointed by South Carolina’s governor three days after senator’s death Darline Graham Nordone: Lindsey Graham’s sister and interim US senator Darline Graham, the sister of the late Republican senator Lindsey Graham, was sworn in to temporarily fill his Senate seat on Tuesday, just three days after his sudden death. stories.title: Young Germans opting out of military service as Berlin strives to boost army

stories.summary: Man from West Midlands taken to hospital after eight-day event in which 57 people were injured An 86-year-old British man is among 57 people injured while taking part in the running of the bulls festival in the northern Spanish city of Pamplona. The man, from Halesowen in the West Midlands, was taken to hospital on Tuesday – the last morning of the eight-day annual event – after suffering injuries to his right hand, left elbow and right eyebrow. stories.title: At least 27 killed in fire at Bangkok pub with another 22 critically injured
stories.summary: The fire is one of the deadliest such incidents in the popular tourist destination in ⁠recent years An explosive fire at a popular pub in Thailand’s ⁠capital, Bangkok, has killed 27 ⁠people and left another 22 ​in critical condition, in one of the deadliest such incidents in the tourism hub in recent years. On Tuesday, the Paris appeals court upheld Le Pen's conviction for misusing European parliament funds but reduced the ban on running for office, clearing the way for the 57-year-old to stand in next year's election Continue reading...

stories.title: Dowry murders in India no longer spark public anger or debate, study finds
stories.summary: Thousands of women are killed in dowry disputes each year, despite the practice being banned in 1961 Dowry deaths in India no longer provoke the public anger they once did, despite thousands of women’s lives still being lost every year, according to new research. The killings – women who are murdered or driven to suicide following dowry disputes between families – have also faded from political debate, despite an increase in cases. Continue reading...

stories.title: Donate now to a Top 10 CNN Hero
stories.summary: Anderson Cooper explains how you can easily donate to any of the 2021 Top 10 CNN Heroes.

stories.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
stories.summary: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

stories.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
stories.summary: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

stories.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
stories.summary: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

stories.title: CNN Heroes: Sharing the Spotlight
The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
stories.summary: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

stories.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
stories.summary: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

stories.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
stories.summary: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

stories.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
stories.summary: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

stories.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
stories.summary: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

stories.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
stories.summary: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

stories.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
stories.summary: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

stories.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
stories.summary: Paul Templer was living his best life.

stories.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
stories.summary: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

stories.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
stories.summary: This is it.

stories.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
stories.summary: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

stories.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
stories.summary: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

stories.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
stories.summary: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

stories.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
stories.summary: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.
